prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. 2013–2014 Atlantic winter storms in Europe were a series of winter storms affecting areas of Atlantic Europe and beyond. The French Atlantic coastal regions (chiefly Brittany), South West and Southern England, West Wales, Ireland, Spanish Atlantic coastal regions (particularly Galicia) were especially affected by a "conveyor belt" series of high-precipitation storms (mostly not exceptional for their winds) and by high tides. Many storms were explosively deepened by a strong jet stream, many deepening below 950 hPa. The repeated formation of large deep lows over the Atlantic brought storm surges and large waves which coincided with some of the highest astronomical tides of the year and caused coastal damage. The low pressure areas brought heavy rainfalls which led to flooding, which became most severe over parts of England such
B. The 2013-2014 Atlantic winter storms were a series of winter storms that affected different regions of Atlantic Europe and beyond. The French Atlantic coastal regions, South West and Southern England, West Wales, Ireland, and Spanish Atlantic coastal regions were particularly affected by a "conveyor belt" of high-precipitation storms and high tides. Many storms were explosively deepened by a strong jet stream, many deepening below 950 hPa. The repeated formation of large deep lows over the Atlantic affected coastal areas of England.
C. Between 2013 and 2014, a succession of winter weather events known as the Atlantic winter storms impacted various parts of Atlantic Europe and adjacent regions. The coastal areas of the French Atlantic (primarily Brittany), the South West and Southern portions of England, Western Wales, Ireland, and the Spanish Atlantic coastline (especially Galicia) experienced significant effects from a continuous sequence of high-precipitation storms (which were generally not notable for their wind speeds) and elevated tides. A powerful jet stream was responsible for the rapid intensification of numerous storms, many of which deepened to below 950 hectopascals. The recurrent development of substantial deep low-pressure systems over the Atlantic Ocean generated storm surges and considerable waves. These phenomena occurred simultaneously with some of the year's highest astronomical tides, resulting in damage to coastal areas. The low-pressure systems delivered substantial rainfall, which subsequently triggered flooding, with the most severe instances occurring across certain parts of England.

Answer:

options.A: 2013–2014 Atlantic winter storms in Europe were a series of winter storms affecting areas of Atlantic Europe and beyond. Many storms were explosively deepened by a strong jet stream, many deepening below 950 hPa. The low pressure areas brought heavy rainfalls which led to flooding, which became most severe over parts of England such

The French Atlantic coastal regions, South West and Southern England, West Wales, Ireland, and Spanish Atlantic coastal regions were particularly affected by a "conveyor belt" of high-precipitation storms and high tides. Many storms were explosively deepened by a strong jet stream, many deepening below 950 hPa. The repeated formation of large deep lows over the Atlantic affected coastal areas of England.
